stimulus: Pulling harder on the strings of a violin does not change the pitch of a note. What changes is the loudness of the sound the strings ______blank a fretted instrument such as a guitar, however, pressing a string against a different fret shortens the vibrating length of the string, which raises the pitch.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is punctuation use between sentences. The period correctly marks the boundary between one sentence (“What...produce”) and another (“On...pitch”).

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a comma splice. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a run-on sentence. Choice D is incorrect because, without a comma preceding it, the conjunction “and” can’t join two main clauses.
